_note: DEPTH-ORACLE ABLATION of baseline_global.json (revenue, seed 42, direct CMA-ES->IX). IDENTICAL except the well-depth search is confined to z in [50, 55] (optimizer.depth_min/depth_max) instead of [5, 62]. Rationale: real revenue rises with depth and SATURATES by z~50-55 (corr(mean_z, real_rev)=0.53 over 1438 baseline IX configs; bins 375M@z35 -> 447M@z50-55, 55-63 no better), and the surrogate-AL's edge over the full-range baseline is almost entirely that it reaches this productive depth immediately (surrogate iter0 mean_z=52.6 vs baseline iter0 z=37.7 crawling to 48 by iter29). This run removes depth as a discovery problem so the optimizer searches xy only. Test question: does the depth-pinned baseline reach the surrogate's ~514M (deep baseline configs already hit 510M) at far fewer IX -> if so, the surrogate+CMA add ~nothing beyond a depth heuristic. Overlay vs local_workspace_baseline_seed42 (full-range) and the cma_clean_6_5 surrogate on the IX-budget convergence plot.
